title: Horizon Europe MAT-PROD-11: Innovative Technologies for Exploration & Data Modelling of Raw Materials
description: Research and innovation action for advanced exploration techniques and data-driven modelling to discover new deposits of critical raw materials in Europe. Supports AI/ML approaches, geophysical methods, and probabilistic geological modelling. Single-stage call under HORIZON-CL4-2026-01.
eligibility: EU-based consortia (minimum 3 entities from 3 EU member states). Non-EU partners can participate as associated partners. Core Element AI can join via EU consortium.
requirements: Consortium formation, technical proposal, work packages, budget, impact assessment, exploitation plan

similar_winners.title: European Rare Earth Element Resource Mapping
AI for Earth Observation in Mineral Exploration

analysis.reasoning: Direct match: AI + exploration + CRM data modelling. Core Element AI's probabilistic subsurface prediction technology is precisely what this call seeks.

title: Horizon Europe MAT-PROD-12: Technologies for Innovative Extraction of Critical Raw Materials
description: Research and innovation action for sustainable and efficient extraction technologies for critical raw materials. Covers mining optimization, AI-driven process control, environmental impact reduction. Single-stage call, TRL 3-5, Lump Sum funding.
eligibility: EU-based consortia (minimum 3 entities from 3 EU member states). Non-EU partners welcome. Core Element AI can join via EU consortium partner.
requirements: Consortium formation, technical proposal, work packages, budget, sustainability assessment

similar_winners.title: Critical Raw Materials Screening and Assessment

analysis.reasoning: Strong match for extraction optimization via AI. Less direct than MAT-PROD-11 but still highly relevant.

title: EIC Accelerator 2026 - Open & Challenges
description: Europe's most competitive deep-tech funding. Up to EUR 2.5M grant + EUR 10M equity. 2026 has 6 batching dates. Step 1 (short proposal) rolling with 4-week evaluation. Challenge: 'Strengthening European critical raw materials supply chain' (EUR 50M).
eligibility: EU-incorporated SMEs/startups. Non-EU companies must establish EU entity. Challenge: 'Strengthening European critical raw materials supply chain' (EUR 50M) highly relevant.
requirements: Step 1: short proposal (rolling). Step 2: full proposal 20 pages (batched). Step 3: interview.

analysis.reasoning: Perfect deep-tech fit. CRM supply chain challenge (EUR 50M) directly targets Core Element AI's domain. Requires EU entity setup.

title: O'Shaughnessy Fellowships & Grants 2026
description: Equity-free funding for builders, researchers, and creatives working on ambitious, world-changing ideas. 10 Fellowships of $100K + up to 20 Grants of $10K+. Fully remote, open worldwide.
eligibility: Adults worldwide. No credentials required. Rolling review within 30 days.

value_props: Ambitious world-changing AI platform for geological discovery

analysis.reasoning: Good fit for founder fellowship. Core Element AI's mission to transform mineral exploration aligns with 'ambitious world-changing ideas'.

title: DARPA I2O Office-Wide BAA (HR001126S0001)
description: DARPA's broadest entry point for AI researchers. The I2O focuses on AI, cybersecurity, autonomy, and information science. Related: CriticalMAAS program transitioning to operational phase in FY2026.
eligibility: US entities. Abstracts required before full proposals. Individual awards $500K-$5M.
requirements: Abstract submission, full technical proposal, budget, team qualifications

value_props: Novel AI approach to geological data

analysis.reasoning: Strong AI + geoscience alignment. CriticalMAAS program validates DARPA interest in AI for mineral assessment. Long deadline allows careful proposal preparation.

title: EIT RawMaterials KAVA Call 13 - Upscaling
description: Accelerates launch of promising technologies in raw materials industry. Targets validated technologies needing testing, demonstration, PoC, and scaling before market. Covers mining, materials, and recycling.
eligibility: Consortia of 2+ partners from different Horizon Europe eligible countries. 30% co-funding minimum.
requirements: Consortium, proposal, budget, TRL validation evidence

value_props: TRL 7 validated technology ready for upscaling

analysis.reasoning: Excellent fit: KAVA 13 specifically targets validated tech needing upscaling — matches Core Element AI's TRL 7 status perfectly. Mining/exploration focus is direct.

title: Emergent Ventures Fellowship & Grants
description: Low-overhead fellowship and grant program by Tyler Cowen supporting entrepreneurs with 'zero to one' moonshot ideas. Non-dilutive grants $1K-$50K. Rolling review with guaranteed response within 2-3 weeks.
eligibility: Open globally. Age 13+. No credentials required.

analysis.reasoning: Quick rolling grant, easy application. Small amount but fast turnaround. Good for supplementary funding or validation.

title: Foresight Institute AI for Safety & Science Nodes 2026
description: ~$3M total annual funding for AI applied to scientific frontiers. Grants typically $10K-$100K. Physical hubs in San Francisco and Berlin.
eligibility: Researchers and innovators working on AI + scientific frontiers.

value_props: AI applied to geological science frontiers

risk_factors: Primary focus is nanotechnology, not geology
analysis.reasoning: Tangential fit — AI for science is relevant but primary focus is nanotech. Worth exploring if geological AI can be framed as scientific frontier.

title: Innovate UK Sovereign AI - Proof of Concept
description: Development of Proof of Concept demonstrators of AI technologies with state-of-the-art performance in areas of strategic importance to UK. Grants GBP 50K-120K.
eligibility: UK registered businesses. May require UK entity establishment.
requirements: Application via Innovation Funding Service. PoC demonstrator plan.

analysis.reasoning: Good AI PoC fit but requires UK entity. Sovereign AI framing could work for geological AI with UK mining sector relevance.

title: DOE Battery Materials Processing and Manufacturing NOFO
description: Notice of Funding Opportunity for battery materials processing and domestic manufacturing. Part of ~$1B in planned DOE critical minerals NOFOs.
eligibility: US entities: companies, universities, national labs, consortia.

partnerships: Direct application as Core Element AI

risk_factors: High competition due to large award size
analysis.reasoning: Massive DOE funding for critical minerals. Watch for NOFO release in Q2 2026. Core Element AI can target exploration/characterization subtopics.

title: ARPA-E RECOVER - Rare Earth and Critical Mineral Recovery
description: Program to develop transformative technologies for recovery of rare earth elements and critical minerals from unconventional sources.
eligibility: US entities including small businesses, universities, national labs.
requirements: Concept paper (when FOA opens), full technical proposal, budget, team

partnerships: Direct application as Core Element AI

analysis.reasoning: Strong fit for AI characterization of mineral deposits. Watch for FOA release in Q2 2026.
